articles.title: Japanese Yen struggles for traction despite weaker US Dollar amid elevated Energy costs
articles.expand: USD/JPY consolidates on Monday after opening the week with a bearish gap as the US Dollar (USD) comes under pressure amid growing optimism that the United States (US) and Iran are moving closer toward a deal that could eventually reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Markets Rally on Iran Deal Hopes as Oil Crashes to $95
articles.expand: Markets began the week trading as if the Strait of Hormuz is already reopening — even though the final agreement has not yet been signed. Investors rushed into a full-scale “peace dividend” trade after reports suggested the US and Iran are edging closer toward a framework agreement that could end the conflict, restore shipping through […] The...

articles.title: The USD is lower as markets react to the peace hopes.
articles.expand: The USD is lower as the US wakes to a holiday and they honor all those who have died on the battlefields, and the UK barbecues get heated up in observance of the May Bank Holiday. With no escalation of the war in Iran and baby steps toward a diplomatic solution for now, hope vs despair is the theme. Pres. Trump is posting on Truth Social that he ha...

articles.title: Gold rebounds as hopes for US-Iran deal weigh on US Dollar, Oil prices
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) rebounds sharply on Monday as hopes for a US-Iran deal to end the war in the Middle East and reopen the Strait of Hormuz weigh on the US Dollar (USD) and Oil prices. At the time of writing, XAU/USD is trading around $4,556, up 1.0% on the day.

articles.title: Iran top negotiator, foreign minister in Doha to work out deal to end conflict - report
articles.expand: The headlines are stating that Iran's top negotiator, also parliament speaker, Ghalibaf and foreign minister Araghchi are in Doha to meet with Qatar's prime minister over a potential deal to end the conflict. Again, I would be remiss if I didn't point out that "potential deal to end the conflict" doesn't exactly mean a final deal between both sides...

articles.title: GBP/USD Price Forecast: Holds onto gains near 1.3500 amid risk-on mood
articles.expand: The British Pound (GBP) clings to opening gains around 1.3500 against the US Dollar (USD) during the late European trading session on Monday. The GBP/USD pair trades higher as the market sentiment remains risk-on due to expectations that the United States (US) and Iran will reach a deal soon.

articles.title: How close are we actually to a US-Iran endgame?
articles.expand: The start of the week is filled with murmurs about a US-Iran deal and some form of agreement. However, what exactly does that entail and what is it really all about? Or better yet, what exactly is it going to lead to? Market players are optimistic in wanting to hear good news but the reality of the situation is that we might not even be close to a ...

articles.title: Euro rises against Canadian Dollar due to lower oil prices, risk-on mood
articles.expand: EUR/CAD extends its winning streak for the fourth consecutive day, trading around 1.6080 during the European hours on Monday.

articles.title: New Zealand Dollar: Seen above 0.60 against US Dollar in 2H26 – ING
articles.expand: ING’s FX Strategist Francesco Pesole expects the Reserve Bank of New Zealand to deliver a hawkish hold at the 27 May meeting, while warning markets underprice the risk of a surprise hike. He sees new projections signalling tightening from 3Q and looks for two 25bp hikes starting in July.

articles.title: USD/CAD Price Forecast: Bullish setup holds, backing case for further gains
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair reverses an intraday dip to sub-1.3800 levels and fills a modest weekly bearish gap, hitting a fresh daily top during the first half of the European session on Monday.

articles.title: Swiss Franc extends its gains as the US Dollar faints amid Iran peace hopes
articles.expand: The Swiss Franc (CHF) accelerates its recovery against the US Dollar (USD) on Monday, with the USD/CHF pair reaching 11-day lows below 0.7820 at the moment of trading, down from highs past 0.7900 last week.

articles.expand: Brown Brothers Harriman’s (BBH) Elias Haddad expects the European Central Bank's (ECB) April Account to underline a tightening bias, with markets already pricing high odds of a June rate hike to 2.25%.

articles.title: Is Iran trying to play to the optics of a deal during the past week?
articles.expand: One of the preconditions for a deal or framework agreement, however you want to put it, is that the US wants Iran to loosen restrictions over traffic through the Strait of Hormuz. articles.expand: The government looks set to extend a helping hand to industries struggling with gas costs, though it’s understood this will be unlikely to take the form of grants.

articles.title: NZ Trade Minister: Need sustainable supply chains to secure energy, fertilizer supplies
articles.expand: Todd McClay, New Zealand's trade and investment minister discusses need for secure supply chains amid the global energy crisis, and the country's recently signed supply chain pact with Singapore. He adds that the country is looking for new trading partners for key fertilizer supplies.

articles.expand: ECB President Christine Lagarde opened the door wider to a June rate hike after signaling that the central bank’s inflation forecasts are likely to move higher again. articles.title: British Pound gains ground above 1.3450 on US–Iran progress
articles.expand: The GBP/USD pair gains traction to near 1.3480 during the early Asian session on Monday. The US Dollar (USD) weakens against the British Pound (GBP) as the United States (US) and Iran signal peace progress. Trading volumes are expected to be light due to a market closure for Memorial Day in the US.

articles.title: EUR/USD Weekly Forecast: Awaiting more war-related headlines and central banks
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair fell for a second consecutive week, settling not far above a fresh multi-week low of 1.1576. War-related headlines kept driving financial markets, coupled with mounting speculation that the United States (US) Federal Reserve (Fed) will deliver a rate hike before the year is over.
